source: SEC EDGAR XBRL frames API via renquant_base_data.sec_fundamentals (weekly refresh cron; FMP income-statement acceptedDate backfills point-in-time availability)
freshness_rule: serving date axis reaches the latest OHLCV price date (resolve_serving_daily_index, decoupled from the alpha158 training-label clip); per-entity quarterly snapshots advance within the ~45d SEC filing lag (fiscal_period_end/available_at ROW-level provenance columns). Per-FEATURE freshness (v2) is a SEPARATE, stricter verdict: each of earnings_yield/book_to_price/gross_profitability/roe/asset_growth carries its own <feature>_source_available_at/_source_fiscal_period_end/_source_age_days, derived from the OLDEST of its actual contributing raw concepts (a ratio is only as fresh as its stalest input, even when carry-forward or a fallback tag made it finite using a DIFFERENT, fresher operand) — consumer gate renquant-pipeline P-FUND-FRESHNESS.
note: Registry entry for the daily serving fundamentals feature feed (earnings_yield, book_to_price, gross_profitability, roe, asset_growth consumed by panel scoring). The authoritative, fingerprinted per-run manifest is written by renquant_base_data.sec_fundamentals.write_daily_ingestion_manifest to <data_dir>/ingestion_manifest_sec_fundamentals_daily.json (fingerprint impl reused from crypto_bars — single impl). IMPUTED-SHARE guard rationale (orchestrator PR #475, 2026-07-11): every non-finite feature cell is silently cross-sectional-median-imputed by the panel scorer, so finite coverage IS the complement of the imputed share; the build WARNS below floor (degrade_with_alarm) and --verify FAILS, so a coverage regression can never again sit dark for months. v2 (2026-07-11, Codex CHANGES_REQUESTED PR #43) adds TWO fixes: (1) coverage denominator — each per-feature entry in feature_coverage now carries BOTH the legacy coverage/n_have/n_expected/denominator (priced_tickers or served_tickers — can look healthy while the OHLCV price cache is badly degraded) AND universe_coverage/n_universe_expected/universe_denominator=declared_scored_universe (the SAME finite-cell count against the FULL declared/scored universe, so a good priced-relative number can never hide a bad one), plus a top-level prerequisite_price_coverage block (priced tickers / declared universe) that caps earnings_yield/book_to_price regardless of ratio-input coverage; (2) provenance freshness — feature_freshness reports, per feature, how many finite cells exceed max_age_days using the per-concept-derived <feature>_source_age_days columns (see freshness_rule). coverage_ok is now the COMBINED axis-health verdict: legacy_coverage_ok AND universe_coverage_ok AND prerequisite_price_coverage_ok AND freshness_ok must ALL pass — alarm/block POLICY on an unhealthy verdict is left to the pipeline/orchestrator consumer, this manifest only emits the honest numbers.
